locations.name: Balboa Park
locations.summary: Historic 1,200-acre urban cultural park with museums, gardens, and the San Diego Zoo
locations.description: Balboa Park is a 1,200-acre historic urban cultural park in San Diego, California. The park contains numerous museums, theaters, gardens, shops, restaurants, and the world-famous San Diego Zoo. Many of the park's attractions are free to explore, including beautiful gardens, walking paths, and architectural landmarks.
locations.special_notes: Park grounds are always free. Some museums and attractions may charge admission. Parking is free on weekends.

locations.name: Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve
locations.address: 12600 N Torrey Pines Rd, La Jolla, CA 92037
locations.summary: 2,000-acre coastal wilderness reserve with rare Torrey pine trees, hiking trails, and dramatic cliff views
locations.description: Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve is one of the wildest stretches of land on the Southern California coast, featuring rare Torrey pine trees, miles of unspoiled beaches, and stunning ocean cliffs. The reserve offers numerous hiking trails with breathtaking views of the Pacific Ocean, making it a paradise for nature lovers and hikers.
locations.special_notes: Parking fee applies ($15-25). Reserve closes at sunset. No dogs allowed on trails. Wear proper hiking shoes.

locations.name: Sunset Cliffs Natural Park
locations.address: 1253 Sunset Cliffs Blvd, San Diego, CA 92107
locations.summary: 68-acre coastal park with dramatic sunset views, rugged cliffs, and ocean vistas
locations.description: Sunset Cliffs Natural Park offers spectacular sunset views over the Pacific Ocean with dramatic coastal cliffs and sea caves. The park features walking paths, scenic viewpoints, and natural rock formations. It's one of San Diego's most photographed locations, especially during golden hour when the cliffs glow with sunset colors.
locations.special_notes: Best visited 1 hour before sunset. No lifeguards. Cliff areas can be dangerous - stay on marked paths. Free parking available.

locations.name: Old Town San Diego State Historic Park
locations.summary: Historic park recreating San Diego life in the 1820s with original adobe buildings and period demonstrations
locations.description: Old Town San Diego State Historic Park preserves and recreates San Diego's early Mexican and American period. The park features historic adobe buildings, museums, restaurants, and shops. Visitors can enjoy free walking tours, watch blacksmith demonstrations, and experience California's early history. The park comes alive with colorful fiestas and cultural events throughout the year.
locations.special_notes: Free park admission. Some museums and restaurants may charge. Free parking available. Cultural events on weekends.

locations.name: Seaport Village
locations.address: 849 W Harbor Dr, San Diego, CA 92101
locations.summary: Waterfront shopping and dining area with street performers, art galleries, and harbor views
locations.description: Seaport Village is a 14-acre waterfront shopping, dining, and entertainment complex along San Diego Bay. While shopping and dining cost money, visitors can enjoy free strolling along the waterfront, watch street performers, browse window displays, and enjoy beautiful harbor views. The area features historic ships, art installations, and regular free events.
locations.special_notes: Free to walk around. Window shopping and street performances are free. Paid parking available. Beautiful sunset views.

locations.name: Gaslamp Quarter
locations.address: 5th Ave & Island Ave, San Diego, CA 92101
locations.summary: Historic Victorian-era district with 16 blocks of dining, shopping, and entertainment venues
locations.description: The Gaslamp Quarter is a 16-block historic neighborhood in downtown San Diego, known for its Victorian architecture, vibrant nightlife, and diverse dining scene. While restaurants and clubs charge admission, the area is free to explore on foot. Visitors can admire the historic architecture, enjoy street performances, and soak in the lively atmosphere of this National Historic District.
locations.special_notes: Free to walk around. Best experienced in evening when district comes alive. Paid parking available. Historic walking tours available for fee.

locations.name: Mission Bay Park
locations.address: 2688 E Mission Bay Dr, San Diego, CA 92109
locations.summary: 4,600-acre aquatic park with beaches, bike paths, and water sports activities
locations.description: Mission Bay Park is a massive 4,600-acre aquatic park offering 27 miles of shoreline, including numerous beaches, picnic areas, and recreational facilities. The park is perfect for biking, walking, jogging, and water sports. Visitors can enjoy free beach access, playgrounds, and beautiful bay views. The calm waters make it ideal for families and water sports enthusiasts.
locations.special_notes: Free park and beach access. Parking fees apply in some areas. Great for families and water activities. Fire pits available for evening use.

priority_locations: Old Town San Diego State Historic Park
